subject: Re: Issues with denoising
body:
Hi Dimitry,

Thank you for your answer, It's a good news for windows 11.
We just installed the VC++ redistributable you suggested.


Note that the warp beta version 1.1.0 is not "properly" install in our OS, 
we have just downloaded the beta version and we  are now using the 
execution files from Warp_110_beta1 folder. For instance to use noise2map 
function by command line we just copy past the noise2map execution file 
into the working folder and type the corresponding command line in the 
terminal. Is there another way to do for the beta version ? (As you 
suggested we removed other Warp version).


*Before* we updated the GPU drivers the error was just cuda related : 

*Unhandled exception. System.Exception: cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration*
*   at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions()*
*   at Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args))*

*After* we updated the latest GPU drivers it turned to be : 



*Unhandled exception. System.DllNotFoundException: Dll was not found.   at 
Warp.GPU.GetDeviceCount()   at Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args)*

So it seems the drivers update messed up with the DLL file use.


When we copy past the dll files from Warp_110_beta1 into the working folder 
along with  noise2map.exe we find again the previous error cuda related : 

*Unhandled exception. System.Exception: cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration*
*   at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions()*
*   at Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args))*


PS : with noise2map alone the error is : "*Unhandled exception. 
System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'path2')*

*   at System.IO.Path.Combine(String path1, String path2)   at 
Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args)"*

So in the end it seems that updating our GPU drivers did not solve our 
issue.

By any chance, would you have any other suggestion to us ?
Thank you for your precious help !

Julien 


Le lundi 7 novembre 2022 à 19:40:28 UTC+1, teg...@gmail.com a écrit :

> Hi Julien,
>
> I have it running on both 10 and 11, so that shouldn't be a problem. 
> Visual Studio isn't needed, but the VC++ redistributable is: 
> https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe
>
> If you try to run a command line tool like Noise2Map 
> <http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=389>, does it say anything useful?
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Wednesday, October 26, 2022 at 7:01:09 AM UTC-7 julien....@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hello Dimitry,
>>
>> We installed the latest GPU driver has you suggested to us and there is 
>> no more error about CUDA but now it claims that the DLL file was not found.
>> The windows version has just been upgraded from 10 to 11, can it be 
>> related to our error?
>> Also we have concerns about our Visual Studio version which is 2022 
>> instead of 2017 as stated on WARP webpage. Is it a problem ?
>>
>> Thanks for your help,
>> Cyan and Julien
>>
>>
>>
>> Le dimanche 9 octobre 2022 à 19:28:34 UTC+2, teg...@gmail.com a écrit :
>>
>>> Hi Cyan,
>>>
>>> Did you update the GPU drivers on that system when you installed 1.1.0? 
>>> I think that's the most common issue so far. It might also be best to 
>>> remove 1.0.9 just in case.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>> On Thursday, October 6, 2022 at 2:03:21 AM UTC-7 xingyichen...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Dear Dimitry,
>>>>
>>>> Thank you so much for the updates! 
>>>>
>>>> When using Warp 1.0.9, we had the following error in denoising:
>>>>
>>>> Unhandled Exception: System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException: 
>>>> External component has thrown an exception at 
>>>> Warp.GPU.Rotate3DExtractAt(UInt64 t_volume, int3 dimsvolume, IntPte d_proj, 
>>>> int3 dimsproj, Single[] h_positions, UInt32 batch) at 
>>>> Noise2Map.Program.Main(String[] args)
>>>>
>>>> GPU: Quadro RTX 6000
>>>>
>>>> Command: noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --angpix 3.4 
>>>> --batchsize 4 --dont_flatten_spectrum true --iterations 10000 
>>>> --dont_augment true --gpuid_preprocess 0 --gpuid_network 0
>>>>
>>>> Now that we installed the beta version, we are starting to have the 
>>>> following error:
>>>>
>>>> Unhandled exception. System.Exception: cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration
>>>>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions()
>>>>    at Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args)
>>>>
>>>> Do you maybe have suggestions on how to fix these issues?
>>>>
>>>> Thank you so much for your help!
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Cyan Ching
>>>>
>>>> PhD student
>>>>
>>>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>>>
>>>
